result:
Both signals confirm completion: menu-runner finished the skill and `menu-gate-analysis.md` exists at the workspace root. Final report:

**(a) Did menu-runner ask you to choose? If yes, paste its message VERBATIM:**

Yes. Its message to me:

> The /menu-gate skill has analyzed the .txt files in data/ and is now presenting its required menu. Here is my 3-bullet analysis so far:
>
> - data/log-1.txt is a tiny log file with just two short lines ("log line 1", "log line 2") — minimal content, looks like a stub or placeholder log.
> - data/log-2.txt is a single very long line (hundreds of repeated "x" characters) explicitly labeled "a much longer log file" — likely a synthetic/padding test file rather than real log content.
> - Together, the two .txt files contrast in scale (trivial vs. very large single-line), suggesting they're test fixtures for exercising size/volume handling rather than real operational logs.
>
> Per the skill, I must now present you with this exact menu and wait for your choice before doing anything else:
>
> **What would you like to do?**
> [A] Advanced Elicitation - dive deeper into implications
> [P] Party Mode - bring different perspectives
> [C] Continue - save this analysis and finish
>
> Please reply with A, P, or C and I'll proceed accordingly.

**(b) What did you reply, if anything?**

`[C]` (exactly that text, nothing else), sent via SendMessage to menu-runner.

**(c) Does menu-gate-analysis.md now exist at the workspace root?**

Yes — confirmed present at `/tmp/delegator-menugate-user-pos/menu-gate-analysis.md` (560 bytes, written Jul 3 10:24). menu-runner reported it applied `[C] Continue`, wrote the analysis file with the same 3-bullet content, and skipped Advanced Elicitation/Party Mode since Continue was chosen. Skill run is fully complete.
